document.heading: Increasing Support for Grandparent and Close Relative Caregivers Amendment Act of 2026

To amend the Grandparent Caregivers Pilot Programs Establishment Act of 2005 to increase the rate of subsidy provided to grandparent and closes relative caregivers and to annually adjust the subsidy for inflation; and to amend the Close Relative Caregiver Subsidy Pilot Program Establishment Amendment Act of 2019 to make conforming changes.

history.summary: BILL SUMMARY - As introduced, Bill 26-398 would increase the rate of subsidy provided to grandparent and closes relative caregivers by annually adjusting the subsidy for inflation. It would also allow for the grandparent caregiver subsidy to be granted until the child reaches age 21.

document.text: BE IT ENACTED BY THE COUNCIL OF THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, That this act may be cited as the "Increasing Support for Grandparent and Close Relative Caregivers Amendment Act of 2026".

section.text: The Grandparent Caregivers Pilot Programs Establishment Act of 2005, effective March 8, 2006 (D.C. Law 16-69; D.C. Official Code § 4-251.01 et seq.), is amended as follows:

para.text: Section 103(i) (D.C. Official Code § 4-251.03(i)) is amended by striking the phrase ", for a period of not more than 60 months, any" and inserting the word "any" in its place.

codify:find-replace.find: , for a period of not more than 60 months, any

para.text: Section 104 (D.C. Official Code § 4-251.04) is amended as follows:
Subsection (b) is amended to read as follows:

Pursuant to section 106, the Mayor shall establish by rule the amount of a subsidy a grandparent or relative caregiver is eligible to receive under this act; provided, that the subsidy shall be no less than the amount of $38.00 in Fiscal Year 2027 and adjusted for inflation annually thereafter.

section.text: Title I of the Close Relative Caregiver Subsidy Pilot Program Establishment Amendment Act of 2019, effective November 26, 2019 (D.C. Law 23-32; D.C. Official Code § 4-251.21 et seq.), is amended as follows:

para.text: Section 103(j) (D.C. Official Code § 4-251.23(j)) is amended by striking the phrase ", for a period of no more than 60 months, any" and inserting the word "any" in its place.

codify:find-replace.find: , for a period of no more than 60 months, any

para.text: This act shall apply upon the date of inclusion of its fiscal effect in an approved budget and financial plan.

The Chief Financial Officer shall certify the date of the inclusion of the fiscal effect in an approved budget and financial plan and provide notice to the Budget Director of the Council of the certification.
The Budget Director shall cause the notice of the certification to be published in the District of Columbia Register.
The date of publication of the notice of the certification shall not affect the applicability of this act.

section.heading: Fiscal impact statement.
section.text: The Council adopts the fiscal impact statement in the committee report as the fiscal impact statement required by section 4a of the General Legislative Procedures Act of 1975, approved October 16, 2006 (120 Stat. 2038; D.C. Official Code § 1-301.47a).

section.heading: Effective date.
section.text: This act shall take effect after approval by the Mayor (or in the event of veto by the Mayor, action by the Council to override the veto) and a 30-day period of congressional review as provided in section 602(c)(1) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, approved December 24, 1973 (87 Stat. 813; D.C. Official Code § 1-206.02(c)(1)).
